title: inula oculus-christi subsp. auriculata
intro: Inula oculus-christi subsp. auriculata, also known as the Golden-eyed Inula, is a flowering plant species in the sunflower family that is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable remedy for various health issues. Its medicinal properties are said to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and supporting immune function. Inula oculus-christi subsp. auriculata can be prepared as herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often used in traditional medicine to treat conditions such as arthritis, ulcers, and respiratory issues.
